items.title: Man charged with five counts of rape following alleged Adelaide Hills break-in

children.content: A man accused of breaking into an Adelaide Hills home and sexually assaulting a person unknown to him has been charged with multiple counts of rape, court documents show.

gallery.altText: An image of the front of the Adelaide Magistrates Court building on Victoria Square

thumbnail.altText: An image of the front of the Adelaide Magistrates Court building on Victoria Square

avatar.alt: A woman with blonde hair wearing a black blazer and white top

items.title: Government doing 'everything in our power' to keep SA safe from notorious paedophile

children.content: The South Australian government has filed two applications to either ensure notorious paedophile and former magistrate Peter Liddy is indefinitely detained or the subject of an extended supervision order.

image.altText: Notorious paedophile and former magistrate Peter Liddy.

items.title: Angus Taylor spruiks gas extraction as SA Liberals oppose fracking bid

children.content: After telling an Adelaide oil and gas conference "we need to get busy digging and drilling", Opposition Leader Angus Taylor bats away questions about the SA Liberal Party's resistance to fracking in the state's South East.

image.altText: A man wearing a suit and tie speaks with a woman in the background and a blue banner for Australian Energy Producers

items.title: 'Grossly intoxicated' lawyer's fatal hit-run crash jail term reduced on appeal

children.content: An Adelaide lawyer who drank 1.1 litres of wine before killing a pedestrian in a fatal hit-run crash has won a reduced sentence on appeal — in part because she has a young child.

gallery.altText: A close up of Lauren Willgoose with a serious expression on her face

thumbnail.altText: A close up of Lauren Willgoose with a serious expression on her face

items.title: Rankine ruled out of final quarter with calf injury as Hawks lead Crows

children.content: Izak Rankine is forced off the ground with a right calf injury as Adelaide begins the last quarter trailing Hawthorn in Launceston. Follow live.

image.altText: Nick Watson celebrates one of his two goals

items.title: Snowtown killer James Vlassakis's image has been revealed. Here's why

children.content: The youngest of the Snowtown killers was the subject of a long-running suppression order that kept his face — or any description of his appearance — hidden for 25 years. Here's why that's been lifted.

image.altText: Snowtown killer James Vlassakis in custody.

items.title: X fights Andrew and Tristan Tate's bid to unmask anonymous critics

children.content: Misogynist influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate have struck a hurdle in their bid to unmask anonymous users they allege have defamed them, with social media platform X asking a Florida court to dismiss the motion.

gallery.altText: two men standing near each other

thumbnail.altText: two men standing near each other

items.title: Outback landholders opening gate to large-scale renewable projects

children.content: If you mention renewable energy to agricultural producers in many regional communities, you will likely ruffle some feathers. But in far west NSW, landholders are showing preliminary support for a planned expansion of solar and wind energy assets.

image.altText: Infant boy sits on gate with his dad in outback arid location. Dad is wearing pink shirt and jeans.

items.title: Widespread infectious disease outbreak labelled public health 'failure'

children.content: The federal government has stepped in to address the largest diphtheria outbreak since records began, unveiling a multi-million dollar package for more vaccines and workers to deliver them.

image.altText: A man in a navy suit and tie looks sternly into camera.

items.title: Man killed mother with tool while under 'supernatural' attack, court hears

children.content: An Adelaide Hills man standing trial for the murder of his mother describes to the Supreme Court how he repeatedly struck her while he was panicked and "under attack" by "supernatural beings".
Warning: This story contains images and details that may be distressing to some readers.

image.altText: A woman speaking to a microphone with her hands on her hips between large grey columns

items.title: New move to change SA's abortion laws goes before state parliament

children.content: South Australia's parliament is set to debate restricting abortion access for the third time in less than two years, with One Nation's breakthrough election result meaning the laws could pass the upper house.

image.altText: Sarah Game smiles next to a group of rally attendees outside a large grey building

items.title: Landscape in outback SA 'permanently changed' by recent earthquake

children.content: Cracks in the ground in outback Australia caused by a magnitude-5.5 earthquake could help researchers predict future seismic activity.

image.altText: Dry, grassy ground with a crack going along the ground through the dirt, with a person in the background

items.title: Green shoots after rain give SA farmers hope after years of drought

children.content: Farmers in the Riverland and Mallee are cautiously optimistic as new growth emerges from the paddock following "meaningful rain".

image.altText: Green sprouts of barley are shooting up through red soil with farmer's boots in backgrounds.

items.title: Hanson proposes tax break for drilling alongside new gas royalty

children.content: The One Nation leader wants a flat royalty on the production of new gas projects.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson in a black turtle neck with her palm pointed out.

items.title: Australia backs landmark UN climate change ruling as others try to block it

children.content: Australia joins 140 other countries in passing a major United Nations resolution backing a landmark legal ruling on climate change, despite efforts by the United States, Russia and Saudi Arabia to sink it.

image.altText: A group of people stand together, raise their fists and hold signs calling for climate justice.

items.title: What is a super El Niño?

children.content: Forecasts are predicting that a "super" El Niño could form later this year.
The severity of these weather patterns tends to depend on ocean temperatures, which we know are rising.
So, there are fears this could be the strongest El Niño ever.
But what exactly is that going to look like for Australia and the world?

image.altText: Image shows a blue background with clouds and white writing over the top that reads: What is a super El Nino?

articles.title: El Niño looms as BOM releases long-range winter forecast

children.content: The Bureau of Meteorology is forecasting winter to be unusually warm across the entire country as an El Niño climate pattern is increasingly likely to take hold.

image.altText: A cloudy day over the water at Williamstown

gallery.altText: Image shows a blue background with clouds and white writing over the top that reads: What is a super El Nino?

thumbnail.altText: Image shows a blue background with clouds and white writing over the top that reads: What is a super El Nino?

items.title: How Australia's EV uptake compares with the rest of the world

children.content: High petrol prices are driving EV sales around the world, with the leading international authority predicting 30 per cent of all new cars sold this year will be electric.

image.altText: workers in a car factory

items.title: Drug testing of athletes to expand as influencers fuel illicit peptides boom

children.content: The nation's anti-doping agency reveals its plans to expand drug-testing in sport in response to the soaring popularity of unregulated peptides in the broader community.

image.altText: A woman holds up two small clear vials.

items.title: As Colbert wraps up The Late Show, late-night TV could be on shaky ground

children.content: Like other checks and balances on presidential power, late-night talk shows — once ratings juggernauts, woven into the fabric of American culture — are being stress-tested. Here's how they became a staging ground for the battle over free speech.

articles.title: Five reasons Stephen Colbert is one of the US's most important satirists

children.content: The best satirists do more than entertain. They influence public discourse and leave lasting marks on political life.

image.altText: Stephen Colbert sits behind a desk on the Late Show set

A woman holds up a protest sign featuring the CBS eye logo and the phrase "Under his eye" outside the Late Show's theatre.
A middle-aged man and an older man, both in suits and wearing glasses, laugh as they sit amongst a crowd in a theatre.

items.title: 'Disgraceful': Israel's ambassador joins criticism of Ben-Gvir

children.content: Israel's ambassador to Australia Hillel Newman has stopped short of apologising to the Global Sumud Flotilla activists detained in Israel – who had been taunted by far-right minister Itamar Ben-Gvir.

children.content: Experts say broken heart syndrome accounts for about two per cent of patients presenting with symptoms similar to a heart attack.

items.title: Israel supported Ben-Gvir's callousness, until it didn't

children.content: Benjamin Netanyahu has condemned a video of his national security minister taunting activists, including Australians, who have been detained. But it's the prime minister who allowed this behaviour to percolate.

articles.title: Israeli minister posts video taunting detained activists of Global Sumud Flotilla

children.content: Some of the detained activists were being pushed and dragged along the ground by Israeli forces while Itamar Ben-Gvir was taunting them.

image.altText: people kneeling on the ground as armed soldiers watch over

articles.title: Government calls for detained Australian flotilla members to be released from Israel

children.content: The Australian government says it is engaging with Israel over the welfare of 11 Australians detained, as Israeli forces fire "non-lethal" shots at two boats in a Gaza-bound aid flotilla.

items.title: Defence minister 'disappointed' by lawsuit over Darwin port ownership

children.content: Chinese-owned company Landbridge launched legal action earlier this year following a pledge by the Prime Minister to return the port to Australian hands. Defence Minister Richard Marles says the government will fight the suit and remained committed to finding a domestic buyer.

image.altText: White man standing in front of podium, light pink shirt, navy blue pants, mid-speech, man behind him in white shirt.

items.title: Elon Musk could become world's first trillionaire as SpaceX files for largest IPO ever

children.content: Elon Musk's sprawling space exploration company, which also owns Starlink and xAI, was valued at $US1.25 trillion ($1.75 trillion) earlier this year, meaning Musk could become the world's first trillionaire if the offering goes ahead at that valuation.

image.altText: SpaceX founder Elon Musk speaks at a press conference

items.title: Why Raúl Castro has been charged now over a plane downing 30 years ago

children.content: The United States has indicted former Cuban president Raúl Castro on murder charges stemming from 1996, reviving one of the lowest points in its tangled history with Cuba.

items.title: Why 70,000 people could decide future for 70 million Brits

items.title: Iran examining US proposal as Trump says talks in 'final stages'
